L1.area: JIT compilation and function object lifecycle management

L2.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the scripting engine's handling of function object types during entry point cleanup. A specific function type is not included when iterating over all registered types, causing stale JIT entry points to persist after garbage collection. An attacker can exploit this via crafted script to achieve remote code execution.

L3.area: JIT compilation and function object lifecycle management
L3.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the scripting engine's handling of function object types during entry point cleanup. A specific function type is not included when iterating over all registered types, causing stale JIT entry points to persist after garbage collection. An attacker can exploit this via crafted script to achieve remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The MapFunctionObjectTypes function omits one of the function types when iterating, leaving stale JIT entry points after cleanup. This leads to memory corruption that can be exploited for remote code execution via crafted JavaScript.
